rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HILL et al VS. DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 5, 2008, line 4. Summary adjudication is granted with respect to the Second Cause of Action for Declaratory Relief. The Court's prior ruling on the demurrer does not affect this cause of action. The Court's decision is based on Parnell v. Adventist Health Systems (2005) 35 Cal.4th 595, 608 that holds that there must be an underlying debt before Dameron may assert a lien on plaintiff's third party recovery under the Hospital Lien Act. As in Parnell, Dameron received payment from plaintiff's health insurer as payment in full for the medical care provided to plaintiff. (Plaintiff's Undisputed Material Facts 1-12.) As a consequence, there is no underlying debt and "the hospital may not recover on the lien even assuming that the recovery comes from the tortfeasor." (Id.) Dameron seeks to come within the exception set forth in Parnell that Dameron and the insurer may "contract for this right." (Id. at 611.) Dameron seeks to establish that it has done so by reliance on extrinsic evidence, citing Wolf v. Superior Court (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1343, 1350. Dameron argument fails. It has not even attempted to point to any provision of the agreement and assert that provision is ambiguous. The Court finds that the key provision, paragraph 6.1, is not ambiguous. It affirmatively states that "Hospital agrees to look solely to BLUE CROSS for payment for Hospital Services, subject only to [certain exceptions]." The exceptions in paragraph 6.1 are inapplicable to this case and this is further evidence that the parties did not contract for this right under the doctrine of inclusio unius est exclusion alterius (the inclusion of one is the exclusion of the other). As a consequence, there is no need to consider extrinsic evidence. It also appears that Dameron is arguing that its course of dealing with Blue Cross modified the contract. To the extent that is the argument, it fails for the contract between Blue Cross and Dameron has an integ

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK VS. PHILIP J NICHOLSEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Pltf'S Motion And Motion To Amend The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 5, 2008, line 5. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK'S Motion To Amend The Complaint. The motion to amend is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff has not demonstrated what additions plaintiff wishes to make. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL R JOHNSON VS. SUCHERMAN & INSALACO LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Michael R. Johnson'S Motion For Leave To File 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 5, 2008, line 13. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL JOHNSON'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is denied. The motion is procedurally defective for improper service and for failing to attach an interlineated proposed amendment as required by CCP Section 471.5. The motion also is without merit under Simmons vs. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001), 92 Cal. App. 4th, 1068. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL R JOHNSON VS. SUCHERMAN & INSALACO LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Consolidate Of Cases
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 5, 2008, line 13. Plaintiff's Motion To Consolidate Cases is off calendar as moot based on the Court's tentative ruling in plaintiff's motions for leave to file second amended complaint. =(302/PJM/JW)
